examples.title: Basic Main Function with I/O
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic structure of a C++ program with main function, header files, and I/O operations
examples.explanation: This is a basic C++ program demonstrating the fundamental structure: include iostream header, use namespace std, define main() function with int return type, use cin for input and cout for output, and return 0 for successful completion.

examples.title: Command-line Arguments Handling
examples.description: Shows how to use argc and argv to handle command-line arguments in C++
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates handling command-line arguments. argc contains the count of arguments, and argv is an array of strings. argv[0] is always the program name. The program validates argument count, converts strings to integers, performs calculation, and returns appropriate exit status.

examples.title: Error Handling with cerr and clog
examples.description: Demonstrates different output streams for error messages and logging
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates the different output streams in C++: cout for normal output (standard output), cerr for error messages (unbuffered, goes to stderr), and clog for logging (buffered). Using the appropriate stream helps distinguish between normal output and error/logging information.

examples.title: Namespace Usage Demonstration
examples.description: Shows both qualified (std::) and unqualified namespace usage in C++
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates namespace usage in C++. The std namespace contains all standard library components. Using std:: prefix (like std::cout, std::string) is the recommended approach as it explicitly shows where each identifier comes from and avoids potential naming conflicts in larger codebases.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is string length for operations
